enrichment.short_description: phpBB is free, open-source PHP bulletin board software for self-hosted online forums, released under the GNU GPL.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: phpBB is free, open-source forum software built in PHP under the GNU GPL, powering self-hosted community forums since 2000.

features.title: Multi-database support
features.description: Runs on MariaDB, Microsoft SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, or Oracle Database.

features.title: Hierarchical subforums
features.description: Flat message structure with support for nested subforums to organize discussions.

features.title: Moderation tools
features.description: Topic split, merge, and lock controls along with user group management.

features.title: Full-text search
features.description: Built-in search across posts and topics.

features.title: Extension system
features.description: Self-contained extensions can be installed through the Admin Control Panel (ACP) to add functionality.

features.title: Modern password security
features.description: Passwords are hashed using Argon2id and Argon2i on supported platforms.

pros: Completely free and open source under GPLv2
Self-hosted, giving full control over data and customization
Mature project with a continuous version history since 2000
Extensible through a plugin/extension system
Supports six major database engines
cons: Requires self-hosting and server/PHP administration
Flat, non-threaded discussion structure feels dated next to modern forum UIs
No official hosted SaaS version — deployment is DIY

faqs.question: Is phpBB free?
faqs.answer: Yes, phpBB is free and open-source software licensed under the GNU GPL v2.

faqs.question: What databases does phpBB support?
faqs.answer: phpBB supports MariaDB, Microsoft SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, and Oracle Database.

faqs.question: Can I extend phpBB's functionality?
faqs.answer: Yes, through self-contained extensions installed via the Admin Control Panel (ACP).

faqs.question: How long has phpBB been around?
faqs.answer: phpBB began in June 2000, with phpBB3 released in December 2007 and ongoing 3.3.x releases since.

use_cases.title: Community forums
use_cases.description: Organizations and hobbyist communities running self-hosted discussion boards.

use_cases.title: Support forums
use_cases.description: Companies hosting free-form community support boards for their products.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is phpBB?
paragraphs: phpBB is free, open-source bulletin board software written in PHP. It began in June 2000 and has been continuously developed since, with phpBB3 released in 2007 and ongoing 3.3.x releases maintained by the phpBB project.
The software is self-hosted and runs on top of a PHP-compatible web server and a supported database engine, including MariaDB, MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, Microsoft SQL Server, or Oracle.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who phpBB is for
paragraphs: phpBB suits organizations, communities, and hobbyists who want a free, fully self-hosted forum they control end-to-end, and who are comfortable managing their own server and PHP environment.

enrichment.short_description: Loomio is collaborative decision-making software for groups to discuss, vote, and record transparent decisions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Loomio helps groups discuss, vote, and document decisions transparently, with plans from $39/month and nonprofit discounts.

features.title: Structured discussions
features.description: Members post questions with background context and relevant documents attached.

features.title: Reasoned voting
features.description: Participants share their position and rationale, surfacing support and concerns.

features.title: Decision records
features.description: Outcomes, reasoning, and next steps stay searchable and accessible after the fact.

features.title: Asynchronous participation
features.description: Groups can contribute to a discussion across time zones and distance.

features.title: Unlimited subgroups
features.description: Pro plan supports unlimited subgroups for larger organizations.

features.title: Regional hosting choice
features.description: Customers can choose loomio.com (USA) or loomio.eu (Netherlands) for data hosting.

pros: Worker-owned, open-source cooperative behind the product
Nonprofit discounts of up to 50% on Pro
Choice of US or EU data hosting
Free trial before committing to a paid plan
Purpose-built for transparent group decisions rather than generic chat
cons: Starter plan is capped at 30 members
No permanent free plan for the hosted service — only a trial
Private Loomio (enterprise) pricing requires contacting sales

faqs.question: Does Loomio have a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Loomio offers a free trial to invite people and make a real decision before subscribing.

faqs.question: How much does Loomio cost?
faqs.answer: Starter is $39/month or $299/year, and Pro is $99/month or $499/year; nonprofits receive discounted rates.

faqs.question: Where is Loomio's data hosted?
faqs.answer: Customers can choose loomio.com (USA) or loomio.eu (Netherlands).

faqs.question: Who runs Loomio?
faqs.answer: Loomio Cooperative Limited, a worker-owned and open-source cooperative based in Aotearoa New Zealand, founded in 2011.

use_cases.title: Nonprofit and cooperative governance
use_cases.description: Groups that need transparent, documented decisions without endless meetings.

use_cases.title: Distributed team decisions
use_cases.description: Organizations that need asynchronous, reasoned decision-making across time zones.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Loomio?
paragraphs: Loomio is collaborative decision-making software that gives groups a dedicated space to discuss, vote on, and document decisions instead of relying on scattered email threads or chat.

Founded in 2011 and run by Loomio Cooperative Limited, a worker-owned and open-source cooperative based in Aotearoa New Zealand, Loomio pairs structured discussion with reasoned voting so groups can see not just what was decided, but why.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Loomio is for
paragraphs: Loomio is aimed at nonprofits, cooperatives, and community or distributed organizations that need transparent, well-documented group decisions and are willing to pay for a hosted or self-managed solution.

enrichment.short_description: Decidim is free, open-source software for citizen participation, participatory budgeting, and democratic assemblies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Decidim is free, open-source software for participatory democracy — strategic planning, budgeting, assemblies, and consultations.

features.title: Participatory budgeting
features.description: Tools for running public budget allocation processes.

features.title: Assemblies and initiatives
features.description: Management of citizen assemblies and initiatives.

features.title: Consultations
features.description: Structured public consultation and deliberation processes.

features.title: Strategic planning
features.description: Modules for organizational and civic strategic planning.

features.title: Transparency safeguards
features.description: Traceability features to keep participatory processes accountable.

features.title: Multilingual support
features.description: Available in 12 languages including English, Spanish, Catalan, French, and Japanese.

pros: Completely free and open source, licensed under Creative Commons BY-SA
Used by major institutions including Barcelona, New York City, Helsinki, Mexico City, and the European Commission
Supports 12 languages out of the box
Backed by an established maintainer, the Associació de Software Lliure Decidim
cons: Requires self-hosting and technical setup like most open-source platforms
No commercial support pricing published on the main site
Built specifically for civic/government participation rather than general business use

faqs.question: Is Decidim free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Decidim is free/libre, open-source software licensed under Creative Commons BY-SA.

faqs.question: Who maintains Decidim?
faqs.answer: The Associació de Software Lliure Decidim, supported by organizations including Barcelona City Council and the Generalitat de Catalunya.

faqs.question: What languages does Decidim support?
faqs.answer: Decidim supports 12 languages, including English, Spanish, Catalan, French, German, and Japanese.

faqs.question: Who uses Decidim?
faqs.answer: Cities and institutions such as Barcelona, New York City, Helsinki, Mexico City, and the European Commission.

use_cases.title: Participatory budgeting
use_cases.description: Local governments running public budget allocation processes.

use_cases.title: Civic assemblies and consultations
use_cases.description: Institutions organizing citizen assemblies and public consultations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Decidim?
paragraphs: Decidim is free, open-source software that lets citizens, organizations, and public institutions self-organize democratically at scale, covering strategic planning, participatory budgeting, assemblies, initiatives, and consultations.

It is licensed under Creative Commons BY-SA and maintained by the Associació de Software Lliure Decidim, with support from institutions such as Barcelona City Council and the Generalitat de Catalunya.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Decidim is for
paragraphs: Decidim is built for city governments, public institutions, and civic organizations that want to run transparent participatory processes, and are already used by cities like Barcelona, New York City, Helsinki, and Mexico City.

enrichment.short_description: Polis is open-source deliberation software that clusters participant opinions with AI instead of using threaded replies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Polis is open-source deliberation software that clusters opinions in real time, with a free Basic tier and a custom Pro plan.

features.title: Statement-based voting
features.description: Participants vote agree, disagree, or pass on statements one at a time instead of replying in threads.

features.title: Opinion clustering
features.description: Machine learning groups participants by response pattern and surfaces cross-cluster consensus.

features.title: Real-time reporting
features.description: Automatic web-based reports generate once a conversation reaches a set threshold.

features.title: Automatic translation
features.description: Statements can be automatically translated for multilingual participation.

features.title: Bulk data tools (Pro)
features.description: Bulk statement uploads via CSV/API and raw data export.

features.title: LLM-enhanced reports (Pro)
features.description: Real-time advanced reports with selectable large language model options.

features.title: On-premises deployment (Pro)
features.description: Available for large organizations that need custom hosting.

pros: Free Basic tier with core reporting and moderation
Open-source project from the Computational Democracy Project
Surfaces cross-group consensus rather than amplifying the loudest voices
Automatic translation of statements
EU data residency option available on Pro
cons: Pro plan pricing is not publicly listed and requires an access request
No threaded discussion or replies by design, which limits some use cases
On-premises deployment is reserved for large organizations on Pro

faqs.question: Is Polis free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Basic plan is free and includes basic reporting, moderation, seed statements, and automatic translation.

faqs.question: How does Polis differ from a normal comment forum?
faqs.answer: Instead of replies, participants vote agree/disagree/pass on statements, and machine learning clusters participants by opinion pattern.

faqs.question: What does the Pro plan add?
faqs.answer: Real-time advanced reports, enhanced LLM reports, bulk CSV/API uploads, EU data residency, and on-premises deployment.

faqs.question: How much does Polis Pro cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is not publicly listed; organizations must submit a request-access application.

use_cases.title: Public policy deliberation
use_cases.description: Governments and institutions gathering structured public input at scale.

use_cases.title: Large-group consensus finding
use_cases.description: Organizations surfacing areas of agreement among large, diverse groups.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Polis?
paragraphs: Polis is an open-source platform for large-scale online deliberation, developed by the Computational Democracy Project. Rather than threaded replies, participants respond to statements one at a time with agree, disagree, or pass, and can submit new statements of their own.

Machine learning clusters participants by their response patterns and surfaces the statements that generate agreement across clusters, helping large groups find consensus rather than amplifying the most vocal participants.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Polis is for
paragraphs: Polis suits governments, policymakers, and civic tech teams that need to gather structured input from large, diverse groups and identify genuine areas of consensus.

enrichment.short_description: Crowdcast is a live-event platform for hosting webinars, workshops, and interactive livestreams with built-in monetization.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Crowdcast hosts live webinars, workshops, and interactive events with chat, polls, and monetization tools from $49/month.

features.title: Customizable Stage
features.description: Branded backgrounds and adaptive layouts for live sessions.

features.title: Gallery
features.description: A preparation space where attendees gather before going live.

features.title: Studio production tools
features.description: Built-in overlays, video, and slide tools for producing sessions.

features.title: Live engagement
features.description: Live chat, polls, and Q&A for audience participation.

features.title: Multistream broadcasting
features.description: Broadcast simultaneously to Facebook Live, YouTube Live, and Twitch.

features.title: Built-in monetization
features.description: Ticketing with Stripe and Patreon integration for paid events.

features.title: Zapier integration
features.description: Connects Crowdcast to other apps for workflow automation.

pros: 14-day free trial with full feature access
Built-in monetization via ticketing, Stripe, and Patreon
Multistreaming to major platforms without extra software
Designed for creators and community builders, not just corporate webinars
cons: Lite plan charges a 5% transaction fee on ticket sales
Session length is capped by plan, from 2 to 6 hours depending on tier
Extra attendees and streaming hours cost additional fees beyond plan limits
Stripe processing fees (2.9% + $0.30) apply on top of plan pricing

faqs.question: Does Crowdcast have a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day trial with all features, capped at 60-minute sessions and 10 live attendees.

faqs.question: How much does Crowdcast cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $49/month (Lite), with Pro at $89/month and Business at $195/month; annual billing saves 30%.

faqs.question: Can I sell tickets to my events?
faqs.answer: Yes, Crowdcast supports ticketing through Stripe and Patreon integrations.

faqs.question: What platforms can I broadcast to?
faqs.answer: Crowdcast supports multistreaming to Facebook Live, YouTube Live, and Twitch.

use_cases.title: Paid online workshops
use_cases.description: Creators monetizing live workshops and classes through built-in ticketing.

use_cases.title: Community livestream events
use_cases.description: Community builders running interactive live shows with chat and polls.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Crowdcast?
paragraphs: Crowdcast is a platform for hosting live events such as workshops, webinars, livestreams, and online conferences, positioned as an alternative to traditional corporate webinar software for creators and community builders.

It combines a branded Stage, a pre-show Gallery, and Studio production tools with live chat, polls, Q&A, and multistream broadcasting to Facebook Live, YouTube Live, and Twitch, plus built-in ticketing through Stripe and Patreon.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Crowdcast is for
paragraphs: Crowdcast targets creators, coaches, and community builders who want to run and monetize live interactive events without stitching together separate streaming, chat, and payment tools.

enrichment.short_description: Butter is a virtual collaboration platform for running interactive workshops, training sessions, and meetings.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Butter is a virtual workshop and meeting platform with a free plan and paid seats from $24/month for interactive sessions.

features.title: Facilitation tools
features.description: Purpose-built tools for running interactive workshops and training sessions rather than plain video calls.

features.title: Unlimited rooms
features.description: Paid plans include unlimited session rooms.

features.title: Session recording
features.description: Record sessions for later review, included on paid plans.

features.title: AI session summaries
features.description: Paid plans can generate AI summaries of sessions.

features.title: Custom branding
features.description: Add custom logos and wallpapers to sessions on paid plans.

features.title: Shared team library
features.description: Build and reuse a shared library of session content.

pros: Permanent free plan for trying the product
Purpose-built for facilitation-heavy sessions rather than generic video calls
AI summary generation available on paid tiers
Backed by over $3M in venture funding
cons: Free plan limited to 60-minute sessions and 20 participants
Free plan's recap history is limited to 7 days
Paid pricing is per seat, which can add up for larger teams

faqs.question: Does Butter have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the free workspace supports sessions up to 60 minutes with up to 20 participants and 5 free team members.

faqs.question: How much do Butter's paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: Paid workspaces are billed per seat, for example a Starter workspace runs from about $24/month per seat.

faqs.question: When was Butter founded?
faqs.answer: Butter was founded in 2020 and is headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark.

faqs.question: Does Butter offer AI features?
faqs.answer: Paid plans include AI-generated session summaries.

use_cases.title: Remote workshops and training
use_cases.description: Facilitators running interactive, collaborative sessions with remote teams.

use_cases.title: Engaging team meetings
use_cases.description: Teams wanting more interactive meetings than standard video calls.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Butter?
paragraphs: Butter is a virtual collaboration platform built for running interactive workshops, training sessions, bootcamps, and other facilitation-heavy sessions, rather than standard one-way video calls.
Founded in 2020 and headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, Butter offers a free workspace plan alongside per-seat paid plans that add unlimited session length, recording, and AI-generated summaries.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Butter is for
paragraphs: Butter is aimed at facilitators, trainers, and remote or hybrid teams that want more interactive, structured sessions than a generic video conferencing tool provides.

enrichment.short_description: WebinarJam is live and automated webinar software for product demos, course sales, and marketing events.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: WebinarJam hosts live and automated webinars for course sales and product demos, with plans from $39/month and a 14-day trial.

features.title: Live webinar hosting
features.description: Real-time presentation tools with interactive engagement features.

features.title: Automated webinars
features.description: Automated scheduling and evergreen replay functionality.

features.title: Multi-speaker support
features.description: Run events with multiple presenters.

features.title: Browser-based joining
features.description: Attendees join with one click, no downloads required.

features.title: Built-in marketing tools
features.description: Features for audience growth and monetization within the platform.

features.title: Scalable infrastructure
features.description: Supports events ranging from small groups to thousands of attendees.

pros: 14-day trial available for $1
30-day money-back guarantee on paid plans
Annual billing discount on all tiers
No attendee downloads required to join a session
cons: Attendee caps and session duration scale with price tier
Entry Starter plan is limited to 100 attendees and 1-hour sessions
Additional team members cost extra above plan allowances

faqs.question: Does WebinarJam offer a trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day trial is available for $1.

faqs.question: How much does WebinarJam cost?
faqs.answer: Plans range from $39/month (Starter, billed annually) to $379/month (Enterprise, billed annually).

faqs.question: Who operates WebinarJam?
faqs.answer: WebinarJam is operated by Genesis Digital LLC, based in Las Vegas, Nevada.

faqs.question: Is there a refund guarantee?
faqs.answer: Yes, WebinarJam offers a 30-day money-back guarantee.

use_cases.title: Course and product launches
use_cases.description: Creators running live sales webinars to launch courses or products.

use_cases.title: Multi-presenter events
use_cases.description: Teams co-hosting webinars with multiple speakers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is WebinarJam?
paragraphs: WebinarJam is live and automated webinar software for hosting online events, product demos, and course sales presentations, operated by Genesis Digital LLC in Las Vegas, Nevada.
It supports both real-time live webinars and automated evergreen replays, with multi-speaker support and browser-based attendee access that requires no software downloads.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who WebinarJam is for
paragraphs: WebinarJam suits marketers, course creators, and sales teams that run webinars to sell products or courses and want built-in monetization and marketing tools alongside the streaming platform.

enrichment.short_description: EverWebinar is automated, evergreen webinar software that replays pre-recorded presentations on a set schedule.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: EverWebinar automates evergreen webinar replays on a schedule or just-in-time, priced from $79/month on a 2-year plan.

features.title: Automated delivery
features.description: Presentations run on scheduled or just-in-time formats, 5-15 minutes after registration.

features.title: Simulated live engagement
features.description: Timed chat messages, polls, handouts, and dynamic attendee counts.

features.title: Hybrid mode
features.description: Pre-recorded video paired with live chat staffing for real-time interaction.

features.title: Replica Replay
features.description: One-click import of existing WebinarJam sessions, including chat history and offers.

features.title: Marketing analytics
features.description: Conversion optimization and funnel testing tools.

pros: $1 trial to test the platform
30-day money-back guarantee
One-click import from WebinarJam sessions including chat history
Multi-year billing options reduce the effective monthly cost
cons: Lowest per-month price requires a 2-year prepaid commitment
Monthly plan is the most expensive option per month at $199
No permanent free plan

faqs.question: Does EverWebinar have a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a $1 trial is offered with no long-term commitment.

faqs.question: How much does EverWebinar cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $199/month billed monthly, $99/month billed annually, or $79/month billed every 2 years.

faqs.question: Who operates EverWebinar?
faqs.answer: EverWebinar is operated by Genesis Digital LLC in Las Vegas, Nevada — the same company behind WebinarJam.

faqs.question: Can I reuse my WebinarJam content?
faqs.answer: Yes, Replica Replay lets you one-click import existing WebinarJam sessions including chat history and offers.

use_cases.title: Evergreen sales funnels
use_cases.description: Marketers running always-on automated webinar funnels without hosting live.

use_cases.title: Scaling reach across time zones
use_cases.description: Businesses serving a global audience without live scheduling constraints.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is EverWebinar?
paragraphs: EverWebinar is automated webinar software that turns pre-recorded presentations into evergreen, always-available events, operated by Genesis Digital LLC in Las Vegas, Nevada — the same company behind WebinarJam.
It delivers presentations on scheduled or just-in-time timing, with simulated live elements like timed chat and polls, and can one-click import existing WebinarJam sessions via Replica Replay.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who EverWebinar is for
paragraphs: EverWebinar fits marketers and course creators who want to run always-on, automated webinar funnels for a global audience without scheduling live sessions repeatedly.

enrichment.short_description: BigMarker is an AI-powered digital experience platform for webinars, virtual, hybrid, and in-person events.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: BigMarker is an AI-powered platform for webinars, virtual, hybrid, and in-person events, with custom pricing via sales.

features.title: Interactive webinars
features.description: Polls, Q&A, reactions, and breakout rooms for live sessions.

features.title: In-person event tools
features.description: Registration, badge printing, check-in systems, and mobile apps.

features.title: Virtual and hybrid events
features.description: Unified experiences for combined in-person and virtual audiences.

features.title: On-demand content
features.description: Video hubs, learning platforms, resource libraries, and community features.

features.title: AI capabilities
features.description: Content generation, personalization, insights, and lead scoring.

features.title: Full-service email marketing
features.description: Built-in invitation and follow-up communications.

features.title: API access
features.description: Available on Enterprise and above, alongside Zapier integration.

pros: Rated 4.7/5 on G2, 4.8/5 on Capterra, and 4.9/5 on GetApp
Supports up to 500,000 live attendees on Enterprise+
Customers include Amazon, Microsoft, Canva, TED, and PMI
Covers webinars, virtual/hybrid, and in-person events in one platform
cons: No public pricing — every tier requires a sales quote
No free trial or free plan advertised on the site
Advanced features like API access, evergreen webinars, and SSO are reserved for higher tiers

faqs.question: How much does BigMarker cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is not published; all tiers (Basic, Enterprise, Enterprise+) require contacting sales for a quote.

faqs.question: How many attendees can BigMarker support?
faqs.answer: Up to 10,000 live attendees on Enterprise and up to 500,000 on Enterprise+.

faqs.question: How long has BigMarker been operating?
faqs.answer: BigMarker's site footer indicates operation since 2010.

faqs.question: Does BigMarker offer AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, including AI content generation, personalization, insights, and lead scoring.

use_cases.title: Enterprise webinar programs
use_cases.description: Large organizations running high-volume webinar and demand-generation programs.

use_cases.title: Hybrid conferences
use_cases.description: Organizations combining in-person and virtual attendees in a single event.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is BigMarker?
paragraphs: BigMarker is an AI-powered digital experience platform for hosting webinars, virtual events, in-person events, and on-demand content, operating since 2010.

It combines interactive webinar tools with in-person event logistics like registration and badge printing, plus on-demand video hubs, learning features, and AI-driven content generation and lead scoring.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who BigMarker is for
paragraphs: BigMarker targets enterprise marketing and events teams running large-scale or hybrid programs, evidenced by customers including Amazon, Microsoft, Canva, TED, and PMI, and support for up to 500,000 attendees.

enrichment.short_description: Airmeet is an AI-powered virtual and hybrid event platform for webinars, conferences, and training programs.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Airmeet powers AI-driven virtual and hybrid events, webinars, and training, with plans from $199/month and a free trial.

features.title: AirGenie AI tools
features.description: AI-generated event summaries, blog posts, and social media content from event data.

features.title: Custom branding
features.description: White-labeling and custom branding for events.

features.title: Live engagement
features.description: Polls, Q&A, and emoji reactions during sessions.

features.title: Breakout rooms and networking
features.description: Dedicated networking lounges and breakout rooms for attendees.

features.title: Gamification
features.description: Leaderboards to drive attendee engagement.

features.title: Large-scale capacity
features.description: Supports up to 50,000 concurrent attendees.

features.title: Mobile apps
features.description: iOS and Android apps for attendees.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: HubSpot integration plus 50+ other app integrations.

pros: Rated 4.6/5 on G2 from 750+ reviews; named a Leader for Enterprise and Mid-Market (Winter 2025)
AI-generated content tools (AirGenie) built into the platform
Free trial with no credit card required
Used by 4,000+ organizations including Comcast, Kaiser Permanente, and Capgemini
cons: Higher-tier Events plan requires a custom quote
Managed Events start at $4,999 per event
Discounted annual pricing requires an upfront commitment

faqs.question: Does Airmeet have a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial is available with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Airmeet cost?
faqs.answer: Premium Webinars starts at $199/month, or $167/month billed annually; the Events tier requires a custom quote.

faqs.question: Does Airmeet have mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, Airmeet offers iOS and Android apps.

faqs.question: What is AirGenie?
faqs.answer: Airmeet's built-in AI tool that generates event summaries, blog posts, and social media content from event data.

use_cases.title: Large-scale webinars
use_cases.description: Organizations running webinars for up to 10,000 attendees.

use_cases.title: Hybrid conferences and training
use_cases.description: Organizations blending in-person and virtual attendees for conferences or training.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Airmeet?
paragraphs: Airmeet is an AI-powered virtual and hybrid event platform for webinars, conferences, townhalls, and training programs, headquartered in Covina, California.

Its AirGenie AI tools generate event summaries, blog posts, and social content from event data, alongside core features like breakout rooms, gamified leaderboards, and support for up to 50,000 concurrent attendees.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Airmeet is for
paragraphs: Airmeet is used by marketing and event teams running large webinars and hybrid conferences, with a customer base of 4,000+ organizations including Comcast, Kaiser Permanente, and Capgemini.
